frozen_components: VSA basis vectors (binary sparse block code dictionary)
architecture.summary: Learn-VRF learns vector-symbolic architecture (VSA) rule formulations for solving Raven's progressive matrices via probabilistic abduction. The system maps attribute values to one-hot probability mass functions (PMFs), translates them to high-dimensional VSA vectors using binary sparse block codes, then learns rule-specific combinations of binding/unbinding operations over these vectors via convex optimization. Each rule generates a confidence score by comparing predicted VSA vectors against ground truth; the final answer is determined either by sampling the highest-confidence rule or taking a weighted combination of all rules, with the candidate panel having the largest cosine similarity to predicted attributes selected as the answer.
trained_components: convex weights for VSA rule formulations (fractional form operands)

biblio.title: Probabilistic Abduction for Visual Abstract Reasoning via Learning Rules in Vector-symbolic Architectures

claims.statement: Learn-VRF achieves an average accuracy of 81.3% when using a sampling-based rule selection and 84.1% with the weighted rule combination on in-distribution I-RAVEN data
Learn-VRF requires only 5k trainable parameters while MLP baseline requires 300k parameters

claims.dataset: I-RAVEN OOD (unseen rule-attribute pairs, center constellation)
claims.statement: Learn-VRF achieves nearly perfect accuracy (99.8–100%) on out-of-distribution unseen attribute-rule pairs, significantly outperforming GPT-3 (65.8–94.2%) and MLP baseline (14.8–75.0%)
Learn-VRF learns VSA rule formulations with just one pass through the training data

The learned rule formulations are interpretable; dominant formulas can be derived by selecting the VSA vector with the highest weight for each term, and algebraic simplifications reveal the equivalent RPM rule

Learn-VRF cannot represent logical rules (e.g., logical OR) on bit-code vectors in the position attribute for 2x2 grid, 3x3 grid, and out-in grid constellations; limited to sums and differences of attributes in VSA fractional form
Learn-VRF reaches 73.5% accuracy on 2x2 grid constellation, approaching the theoretical upper bound of 76.1% (share of non-logical rules)
Learn-VRF outperforms pure connectionist baselines (MLP) and large language models (GPT-3) on out-of-distribution tests while maintaining transparency and interpretability
